propositions: The bot writes a heartbeat timestamp file once per 15s poll; freshness must stay under 15s and age over 90s is treated as lander_down.
A stat-based HEALTHCHECK on the local heartbeat file marks the container unhealthy when age exceeds 90s and must not probe Gerrit to avoid restart flapping on outages.
An emergency-stop sentinel file on the state volume pauses the bot (transition to paused phase) and stops consuming new Autosubmit changes; it persists across redeploys.
On SIGTERM the bot first writes recovery.json (wipChain snapshot) then attempts a graceful finish bounded to 120s, backed by stop_grace_period: 120s.
During drain the bot keeps heartbeating each tick so autoheal is not provoked into a mid-drain docker restart that would ignore stop_grace_period.
On restart the bot reconciles recovery.json against live Gerrit and S3 markers before selecting new work, ensuring no double-submit and no stranded stack across all phases and incomplete hand-backs.
